name: Hash Table
def: A map from keys to values giving expected O(1) lookup via a hash function and bucket array.
problem: Constant-time membership and key→value retrieval over an unordered domain.
mechanism: Hashes the key to a bucket index; collisions resolved by chaining or open addressing. Load factor triggers rehash into a larger table.
benefits: Simple, ubiquitous, well-optimised in std libs
limits: Worst case O(n) under adversarial/poor hashing
Rehash spikes; pointer-chasing in chained variants
failures: Hash flooding (algorithmic complexity DoS) with attacker-chosen keys
Mutating a key after insertion corrupts the bucket
whenNot: Range queries or ordered iteration needed (use a tree/B-tree)
Tiny n where a linear scan beats hashing overhead
myths: "O(1) always" — only expected/amortised; depends on hash quality and load factor
